buggy_code:
with open('a.txt') as f1, open('b.txt') as f2:
    data = f1.read()
data2 = f2.read()
task: Read both files inside the context manager block
error_location: line 3: f2.read() called outside the with block after f2 is closed
clarification_query: Is f2 still open after the with block exits?
clarification_options: no, both files are closed when the with block exits
expected_user_response: no, both files are closed when the with block exits
corrected_code:
with open('a.txt') as f1, open('b.txt') as f2:
    data = f1.read()
    data2 = f2.read()
explanation: The with statement closes all managed files on exit. Reading f2 outside the block raises ValueError (I/O on closed file).
